category_title: Preschoolers with frequent inappropriate social behaviors (yelling, taking toys, crying)
student_description: Gregory is a preschooler who engages in high-frequency disruptive social behaviors, such as taking toys and yelling, particularly during routine changes. He does not respond well to reactive rule rehearsals, uniquely requiring proactive pre-correction immediately before transitions, concrete visual feedback, and structured choice-based reinforcement.

criteria.rationale: Having Gregory actively count his daily visual feedback builds self-monitoring and ownership of his behavior.
criteria.check: The long-term plan includes a structured routine (e.g., end of day) where the student actively reviews or counts his accumulated visual feedback.

criteria.rationale: Gregory is highly motivated by earning specific, personalized activities from a choice board when he meets his overall behavioral goals.
criteria.check: The long-term plan incorporates a choice board of preferred activities (e.g., nature walk, computer time) contingent on a mostly positive behavioral review.

criteria.rationale: To sustain positive behavior and reduce dependency on daily rewards, Gregory requires a structured plan to thin his reinforcement schedule.
criteria.check: The long-term plan includes a specific strategy to fade the frequency of rewards (e.g., moving from daily to weekly) once behavior stabilizes.
